title: Webhooks, Replay Defense, SSRF, Unsafe Consumption, and Business Abuse · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A backend pairing room predicts runtime, request, middleware, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first signed ingress, bounded egress, and abuse decision model for webhooks, replay defense, ssrf, unsafe consumption, and business abuse. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of signed ingress, bounded egress, and abuse decision model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Verify provider identity, algorithm, exact bytes, timestamp freshness, constant-behavior signature comparison, key rotation, and failure disclosure before parsing.
Deduplicate stable delivery identifiers, persist acceptance atomically, return promptly, process asynchronously, and classify retryable provider responses.
Allowlist scheme and port, canonicalize host, resolve and revalidate every destination, block private or metadata ranges, control redirects, and enforce egress network policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Webhook Signature Freshness
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 1778 in a recovery drill following an uncertain transaction commit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine signed ingress freshness, replay defense, and SSRF egress policy; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then make OpenAPI and runtime disagree on nullability and block the release. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Webhook Signature Freshness.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for verify provider identity, algorithm, exact bytes, timestamp freshness, constant-behavior signature comparison, key rotation, and failure disclosure before parsing. For Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Webhook Signature Freshness, keep Webhook Signature Freshness connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

hints: For Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Business Flow Abuse, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Business Flow Abuse precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Business Flow Abuse, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop team handles case 3204 in a recovery drill following an uncertain transaction commit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine signed ingress freshness, replay defense, and SSRF egress policy; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model automation-sensitive business operations with actor, cost, inventory, velocity, sequence, idempotency, anomaly, friction, appeal, and recovery evidence.

For Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Business Flow Abuse, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: A generic per-IP rate limit prevents every form of automated business-flow abuse.

stimulus.title: Business Flow Abuse failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A generic per-IP rate limit prevents every form of automated business-flow abuse.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model automation-sensitive business operations with actor, cost, inventory, velocity, sequence, idempotency, anomaly, friction, appeal, and recovery evidence.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Business Flow Abuse happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output. This does not satisfy Business Flow Abuse for signed ingress, bounded egress, and abuse decision model.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Business Flow Abuse condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Business Flow Abuse output and hide the violated signed ingress, bounded egress, and abuse decision model assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Business Flow Abuse.

steps.title: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Business Flow Abuse
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Business Flow Abuse: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop team handles case 8061 in a recovery drill following an uncertain transaction commit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine signed ingress freshness, replay defense, and SSRF egress policy; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then reuse an idempotency key with different input and reject the fingerprint mismatch. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model automation-sensitive business operations with actor, cost, inventory, velocity, sequence, idempotency, anomaly, friction, appeal, and recovery evidence.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Business Flow Abuse.
steps.why: Retrieving Business Flow Abuse through the concrete signed ingress, bounded egress, and abuse decision model case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Business Flow Abuse, keep Business Flow Abuse connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Model automation-sensitive business operations with actor, cost, inventory, velocity, sequence, idempotency, anomaly, friction, appeal, and recovery evidence.
